title: Smoky Crispy Middle Eastern Chicken Kebabs
description: Succulent chicken cubes marinated in smoky spices, grilled to a crisp finish, and served with a fresh parsley and lemon zing.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, cayenne (if using), black pepper, low-sodium salt, lemon juice, and chopped parsley.
Add the chicken cubes to the bowl and toss until fully coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes (up to 4 hours) to marinate.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat (about 400°F/200°C). If using charcoal, allow the coals to reach medium-hot.
Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers, spacing evenly. Soak wooden skewers in water for 30 minutes beforehand to prevent burning.
Place the skewers on the grill, leaving a small gap between each. Grill for 4–5 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until chicken is charred and reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
While grilling, lightly brush each kebab with a splash of olive oil to enhance crispness.
Once cooked, remove the kebabs and let them rest for 2 minutes.
Arrange kebabs on a platter, drizzle with an extra splash of lemon juice and sprinkle additional fresh parsley on top.
Garnish with a bay leaf on the side or tuck it among the kebabs for a fragrant smoky touch.
Serve hot with warm flatbread or a side of cucumber‑yogurt salad.
